title: Use temporally ordered hierarchical target encoders for Driver/Race/Compound interactions
summary: Driver and race IDs are very high-cardinality and likely carry stable strategy priors, but raw codes/frequencies waste signal and naive target means leak.
rationale: Driver and race IDs are very high-cardinality and likely carry stable strategy priors, but raw codes/frequencies waste signal and naive target means leak. Regularized target encoding with GLMM-style shrinkage performs well on high-cardinality data, and CatBoost-style ordered statistics show how to compute such signals without leakage.
implementation_hint: Build out-of-fold encoders with partial pooling for keys like Driver, Driver+Race, Driver+Compound, Race+Compound, and Driver+Race+Stint, using only prior folds or prior chronological rows; encode with posterior mean plus count/uncertainty features.
expected_effect: Should strengthen categorical signal for rare drivers/races while avoiding the brittleness of plain factor codes and global frequencies.
risk: Improper encoding order will silently leak target information; overly aggressive interaction keys can become sparse noise.
